svg.content: <mxfile host="app.diagrams.net" agent="Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.15; rv:147.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/147.0" version="29.6.1"> <diagram name="Page-1" id="WyqsgYrrI0WtuR6lodVu"> <mxGraphModel dx="3249" dy="1974" grid="1" gridSize="10" guides="1" tooltips="1" connect="1" arrows="1" fold="1" page="1" pageScale="1" pageWidth="850" pageHeight="1100" math="0" shadow="0"> <root> <mxCell id="0" /> <mxCell id="1" parent="0" /> <mxCell id="ioHQ6Q7ugxvHsduZXjgB-1" parent="1" style="shape=image;verticalLabelPosition=bottom;labelBackgroundColor=default;verticalAlign=top;aspect=fixed;imageAspect=0;image=data:image/png,;" value="" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="630" width="1997.84" x="456" y="200" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="ioHQ6Q7ugxvHsduZXjgB-2" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;overflow=hidden;rounded=0;" value="<h1 style="margin-top: 0px;"><font style="font-size: 26px;"><span id="allowed-text">Sprint 1: Core Foundation</span></font></h1><h1 style="margin-top: 0px;"><font style="font-size: 20px;">Goal: Create a basic app with recipe CRUD functionality that works from beginning to end.</font></h1><p><font style="font-size: 18px;">Plan (Analyse): Review the requirements for a minimal viable recipe (title, ingredients, instructions, photo), create a SQLite database with indexes for efficient searching, connect user stories to MVP features, and estimate team velocity based on previous projects.<br><br>Design: Create a single-page app layout (header + recipe form + list view), build a responsive TailwindCSS grid for recipe cards (title overlay on thumbnail), and wireframe picture upload with preview thumbnail generation via Pillow resize (200x200).<br>​<br><br>Setup PyWebView window to serve local index.html. Implement RecipeModel class with create(), read(), and list_all(limit=20) methods using SQLAlchemy. Bridge JS form data to Python via window.api.save_recipe(JSON.stringify(formData)). Persist images with unique UUID filenames.<br><br>Pytest unit tests database operations (insert/select/update/delete cycles), manual verification form saves and displays correctly, and page load time &lt;2s with 10 recipes.</font><br><font style="font-size: 18px;">​</font></p><p><br></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="680" width="490" x="656" y="880" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell letter-spacing: normal; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; word-spacing: 0px; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; white-space: normal; background-color: oklch(0.2009 0.003 67.68); text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;"></h2><h1 style="margin-top: 0px;"></h1><div><font style="font-size: 20px;">Goal: Recipe management that is production-ready, including search and nutrition.</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 18px;"><br></font></div><font style="font-size: 18px;">Plan (Analyse): Prioritise high-value features from Sprint 1 feedback (search/filter &gt; gallery), break down nutrition parser for API/manual data, and create caching layer for repeated queries using Python functools.lru_cache.<br><br>Design: UI expansion with search bar (debounced input), filter chips (diet/cuisine), nutrition modal popup with Chart.js doughnut (calories/macros) and infinite scroll loader animation with CSS keyframes.<br>​<br><br>Implement full-text search with SQL LIKE '%query%' on indexed columns or Pandas vectorised string matching, integrate Spoonacular API (requests.get(f"https://api.spoonacular.com/recipes/complexSearch?query={term}&amp;apiKey={key}")), parse response to populate nutrition fields, and implement JS gallery carousel with touch-swipe support.<br>​<br><br>Integration tests for API fallback (manual entry works when API fails), search accuracy tests (80% recall on test dataset), and cross-device responsive testing (mobile/desktop).</font><p><br></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="680" width="490" x="1266" y="880" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="ioHQ6Q7ugxvHsduZXjgB-4" parent="1" text-decoration-color: initial;"></h2><h1 style="margin-top: 0px;"></h1><div><font style="font-size: 20px;">Goal: Complete the app with deployment-ready features.</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 18px;"><br></font></div><font style="font-size: 18px;">design (Analyse): Synthesise Sprint 1-2 learnings (drag-drop UX pain points), integrate meal planner + exports into the roadmap, design capacity for a 2-week buffer, including a buffer for bugs/unexpected issues.<br><br>Design: Meal planner as a 7x3 grid (days x meals) with HTML Drag API zones, shopping list organised by category (produce/dairy) with checkboxes, and PDF export layout that matches printable grocery format.<br><br>Develop drag-drop handlers mapping recipe IDs to meal slots (window.api.assign_meal('2026-02-20', 'lunch', recipe_id)), implement consolidator algorithm (dict merge + fuzzywuzzy ratio&gt;80% for duplicates like "milk" vs "whole milk"), generate PDF via ReportLab canvas with recipe photos/quantities, PyInstaller spec file (--onefile --windowed --add-data "web;web").<br><br>Test: Complete end-to-end process (add recipes → plan week → export list), performance with 50 recipes, PyInstaller validation on clean VMs, and user testing with 3 peers for UX feedback.</font><p><br></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="680" width="490" x="1816" y="880" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="ioHQ6Q7ugxvHsduZXjgB-14" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=default;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;" value="<div 36px;"><br></font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Search and import recipes</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Manual recipe entry&nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">View photo gallery (recipe cards + carousel)</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Generate weekly meal plans</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Auto-create shopping lists</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Nutrition breakdown (calories/macros)</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Dietary filters (vegan, keto)</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">“What’s in my fridge” suggestion</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Export grocery list as PDF</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;"><br></font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;">Non-Functional Requirements:</font></div><div style=""><font style="font-size: 36px;"><br></font></div><div><font style="font-size: 36px;"><div style="">Responsive UI</div><div style="">Clean modern design&nbsp;</div><div style="">Fast performance</div><div style="">SQLite data storage</div><div style="">Executable packaging using div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 488px; height: 1px; padding-top: 687px; margin-left: 268px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 676px; overflow: hidden; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

h1.font: Goal: Create a basic app with recipe CRUD functionality that works from beginning to end.

p.font:
Plan (Analyse): Review the requirements for a minimal viable recipe (title, ingredients, instructions, photo), create a SQLite database with indexes for efficient searching, connect user stories to MVP features, and estimate team velocity based on previous projects.
Design: Create a single-page app layout (header + recipe form + list view), build a responsive TailwindCSS grid for recipe cards (title overlay on thumbnail), and wireframe picture upload with preview thumbnail generation via Pillow resize (200x200).
​
Setup PyWebView window to serve local index.html. Implement RecipeModel class with create(), read(), and list_all(limit=20) methods using SQLAlchemy. Bridge JS form data to Python via window.api.save_recipe(JSON.stringify(formData)). Persist images with unique UUID filenames.
Pytest unit tests database operations (insert/select/update/delete cycles), manual verification form saves and displays correctly, and page load time <2s with 10 recipes.

div.font: Goal: Recipe management that is production-ready, including search and nutrition.

Plan (Analyse): Prioritise high-value features from Sprint 1 feedback (search/filter > gallery), break down nutrition parser for API/manual data, and create caching layer for repeated queries using Python functools.lru_cache.
Design: UI expansion with search bar (debounced input), filter chips (diet/cuisine), nutrition modal popup with Chart.js doughnut (calories/macros) and infinite scroll loader animation with CSS keyframes.
​
Implement full-text search with SQL LIKE '%query%' on indexed columns or Pandas vectorised string matching, integrate Spoonacular API (requests.get(f"https://api.spoonacular.com/recipes/complexSearch?query={term}&apiKey={key}")), parse response to populate nutrition fields, and implement JS gallery carousel with touch-swipe support.
​
Integration tests for API fallback (manual entry works when API fails), search accuracy tests (80% recall on test dataset), and cross-device responsive testing (mobile/desktop).

div.font: Goal: Complete the app with deployment-ready features.

design (Analyse): Synthesise Sprint 1-2 learnings (drag-drop UX pain points), integrate meal planner + exports into the roadmap, design capacity for a 2-week buffer, including a buffer for bugs/unexpected issues.
Design: Meal planner as a 7x3 grid (days x meals) with HTML Drag API zones, shopping list organised by category (produce/dairy) with checkboxes, and PDF export layout that matches printable grocery format.
Develop drag-drop handlers mapping recipe IDs to meal slots (window.api.assign_meal('2026-02-20', 'lunch', recipe_id)), implement consolidator algorithm (dict merge + fuzzywuzzy ratio>80% for duplicates like "milk" vs "whole milk"), generate PDF via ReportLab canvas with recipe photos/quantities, PyInstaller spec file (--onefile --windowed --add-data "web;web").
Test: Complete end-to-end process (add recipes → plan week → export list), performance with 50 recipes, PyInstaller validation on clean VMs, and user testing with 3 peers for UX feedback.
